landing.title: Your life as a
landing.placeholder: See your life from a new perspective
landing.heroSubtitle: Every week from birth to death. Green = you wrote code. Gray = you didn't. Type a username in the search bar or sign in to include your private commits.
landing.demoError: Could not load data for this user
landing.famousSubtitle: Same grid, real GitHub data. See how Torvalds, Karpathy, and 28 others look on the life scale.
landing.whatIsThisDesc1: The "life in weeks" idea has been around for a while — an entire human life fits into a grid of ~4,000 cells. GitLife overlays real commit history from any GitHub profile on top.
landing.whatIsThisDesc2: Type a username in the search bar — the grid is ready. Sign in to include your private commits. The calculator refines your estimate with 21 factors.
landing.featureVisualizationDesc: Your whole life as a grid. Weeks, months, or years.
landing.featureGithubDesc: Your commit history mapped onto your life timeline.
landing.featureCalculatorDesc: Life expectancy calculator. Each factor links to a DOI source.
landing.featureDataDesc: Average life expectancy for 217 countries. Real statistics.
landing.featureCompareDesc: Torvalds, Karpathy, Evan You, Dan Abramov and 26 others. Browse their life grids with real GitHub data.
landing.featureOpenSourceDesc: MIT license. No ads, no tracking. Fork it, run locally, or use the free hosted version.
landing.featureI18nDesc: en, zh, es, pt, ru, de, ja. Add yours via PR.
landing.ctaSubtitle: Free, no sign-up required. GitHub login is only needed for private commits.

settings.title: Settings
settings.birthDateDescription: Your date of birth is used to calculate the life grid.
settings.manualDescription: Set your expected age manually with the slider below.

calculator.impactPreview: Fill in factors to see their impact
calculator.baselineAge: Baseline life expectancy for your country
calculator.resultExplainer: Based on real average life expectancy for your country and gender (World Bank data, 217 countries) adjusted by your personal health and lifestyle factors from peer-reviewed studies.

about.title: About GitLife
about.heroSubtitle: A side project that started with a question: what does a developer's entire life look like?
about.originP1: The idea of visualizing your life as a grid of weeks has been floating around the internet for years — bots, articles, posters. One image: your entire life on a single page, ~4,000 cells.
about.originP2: GitHub's contribution graph shows activity for the past year. GitLife stretches that idea across your entire life — from birth to estimated death.
about.originP3: That's GitLife. No grand mission, no startup pitch. Just a different way to look at your time.
about.dataP1: Public commits are fetched via GitHub API by username. Sign in with GitHub to include private commits too. No access to your code or repositories.
about.dataP2: The life expectancy calculator uses 21 factors from epidemiological studies. Every factor links to its DOI — a permanent link to the published paper. You can verify every number.
about.dataP3: Country-level baseline life expectancy comes from World Bank data (217 countries).
about.privacyP1: No analytics. No ads. No tracking pixels. Your health data is stored in your browser's database and never leaves your device.
about.privacyP2: The code is MIT licensed and public on GitHub. You can read exactly what it does.
about.techP1: Next.js 16 (App Router), Better Auth (GitHub OAuth), Drizzle ORM, Neon Postgres, shadcn/ui v4, Tailwind CSS v4, next-intl.
about.techP2: 7 languages: English, Chinese, Spanish, Portuguese, Russian, German, Japanese.
about.contributeP1: GitLife is open source and built to be easy to contribute to. Here's what you can do:
about.contributeAdd: Add famous developers — the list is a JSON array with GitHub username, birth year, and country. If someone is missing — add them.
about.contributeTranslate: Add a language — each translation is one JSON file in messages/. Copy any existing one and translate.
about.contributeFactors: Improve life expectancy factors — find a peer-reviewed study with a DOI? Add a new factor or refine an existing one.
about.contributeFix: Fix bugs — found something broken? Grid rendering, mobile layout, wrong data — PRs for fixes are always welcome.
about.contributeFeatures: Build features — scale selector, better tooltips, data export, new visualizations. Check the issues for ideas or propose your own.
about.contributeP2: The whole codebase is standard Next.js + TypeScript. Data lives in plain JSON files. No complex setup needed.
